message:
fix: Handle in/not in operators in list comprehension conditions

The genExprWithSubs function was falling back to " ? " for .In and .NotIn
comparison operators, generating invalid Zig code like "(o ? list)".

Now delegates to main genExpr for complex comparisons (in, not in, is, is not)
which properly generates runtime.pyContains() and other correct code.

Before: if ((o ? _UNSHAREABLE_SIMPLE))
After:  if (((!runtime.pyContains(T, list.items, o))))
